title: Remove duplicate entries from images JSON output
body:
This change is a follow-up to 7c2467c to use the break outer
construct and only display a single JSON block per image.

Closes: #1259
Signed-off-by: Tristan Cacqueray <tdecacqu@redhat.com>

comments.body: Test failure is a network flake, going to rekick.
Pull-request updated, HEAD is now e25d720c08682d4d8aed3f394f0d954ae59bede2
@TomSweeneyRedHat sure thing, e25d720 adds a test. Out of curiosity, is there a way to run just a single test?

@TristanCacqueray you can do:  `./tests/test_runner.sh images.bat`.  The only downside to that is there are sometimes a lot of test within each bats file.  In that case I'll copy the file to a new file, remove all the old tests (be sure to keep the first 3 or four lines up through the load), then just have my new test in there for real quick testing.
Seems like policy.json has disappeared from travis?
@TristanCacqueray your tests are failing since you are not specifying the policy.json.
@TristanCacqueray buildah commit also needs the signature policy flag.

:pushpin: Commit 0395c4f has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan 0395c4fe95970c48ba2542156e74ba00c36b0e65 -->

Last 2 run failed on "not ok 218 remove multiple images" with go1.11 and BUILDAH_ISOLATION=rootless... Though the first failure happen when pulling alping, the other when pulling busybox. Wouldn't using scratch be better for that test?

:sunny: Test successful - [status-papr](https://s3.amazonaws.com/aos-ci/ghprb/projectatomic/buildah/d31630b223f767ecb0d8163ec004180d87b595a5.1546773113269811968/index.html), [status-travis](https://travis-ci.org/containers/buildah/builds/475948156?utm_source=github_status&utm_medium=notification)
Approved by: rhatdan
Pushing d31630b223f767ecb0d8163ec004180d87b595a5 to master...
